2604.05539.title: From Large Language Model Predicates to Logic Tensor Networks: Neurosymbolic Offer Validation in Regulated Procurement
2604.05539.paper_summary: We present a neurosymbolic approach, i.e., combining symbolic and subsymbolic artificial intelligence, to validating offer documents in regulated public institutions. We employ a language model to extract information and then aggregate with an LTN (Logic Tensor Network) to make an auditable decision. In regulated public institutions, decisions must be made in a manner that is both factually correct and legally verifiable. Our neurosymbolic approach allows existing domain-specific knowledge to be linked to the semantic text understanding of language models. The decisions resulting from our pipeline can be justified by predicate values, rule truth values, and corresponding text passages, which enables rule checking based on a real corpus of offer documents. Our experiments on a real corpus show that the proposed pipeline achieves performance comparable to existing models, while its key advantage lies in its interpretability, modular predicate extraction, and explicit support for XAI (Explainable AI).
2604.05539.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種神經符號方法，即結合符號和亞符號人工智慧，以驗證受監管公共機構的報價文件。我們使用語言模型來提取信息，然後與 LTN（邏輯張量網絡）進行聚合，以做出可審計的決策。在受監管的公共機構中，決策必須以事實正確且法律可驗證的方式進行。我們的神經符號方法允許將現有的特定領域知識與語言模型的語義文本理解相連接。我們的流程所產生的決策可以通過謂詞值、規則真值和相應的文本段落來進行辯護，這使得基於真實的報價文件語料庫進行規則檢查成為可能。我們在真實語料庫上的實驗顯示，所提出的流程達到了與現有模型相當的性能，而其主要優勢在於可解釋性、模塊化謂詞提取以及對 XAI（可解釋人工智慧）的明確支持。
2604.05539.author: Cedric Haufe et.al.
